cot_prefix: Okay, let's see here. Jane quit her job on March 20, 2020. 176 days have passed since then. We need to find out what the date will be tomorrow in MM/DD/YYYY format, and then pick the correct option from the given choices. Alright, first step is to figure out the date exactly 176 days after March 20, 2020, and then add one more day to get tomorrow's date. Let me start by calculating the date that is 176 days after March 20, 2020. March has 31 days, so from March 20 to March 31, that's 11 days (since the 20th is included, but I need to count the days after that). So from March 20 to March 31 is 11 days. Then April has 30 days, May has 31, June has 30, July has 31, August has 31, September has 30, and so on. Let me count the days step by step: Starting from March 20, 2020. March 20 to March 31: 11 days (since 31 - 20 = 11, but including the 20th or not? Wait, if she quit on March 20, then the first day after quitting would be March 21. So from March 21 to March 31 is 11 days. So that's 11 days. Then April has 30 days. So 11 + 30 = 41 days so far. May has 31 days. 41 + 31 = 72 days. June has 30 days. 72 + 30 = 102 days. July has 31 days. 102 + 31 = 133 days. August has 31 days. 133 + 31 = 164 days. Now, we have 164 days accounted for up to August 31, 2020. We need to reach 176 days. So 176 - 164 = 12 days. So 12 days into September. September 1 is the first day, so September 12 would be the 12th day. Wait, no. If August 31 is day 164, then adding 1 day would be September 1. So adding 12 days would be September 12. Wait, no. Wait: 164 days is August 31. Then adding 1 day is September 1 (day 165), and adding 12 days would be September 12 (day 164 + 12 = 176). So the date 176 days after March 20, 2020 is September 12, 2020. But the question says "What is the date tomorrow in MM/DD/YYYY?" So if 176 days have already passed since March 20, 2020, then the current date is September 12, 2020. Therefore, tomorrow would be September 13, 2020. Which is 09/13/2020. Let me check the options.
